archetype: ARCHETYPE-3 (Stateful Runtime / Plugin Runtime) for the primary surface — the worker consumer is a stateful at-least-once message runtime owning delivery guarantees and an applied-keys store, exactly the archetype whose F-13 saga/runtime-invariants and idempotency doctrine apply.
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (plugins/workers is a deployable plugin service runtime; composition-root wiring + graceful behavior in scope)
lockedDecisions: Store substrate is the shared getKv() WatchableKv handle (KvStore/atomic? + set expireIn), NOT raw Deno.openKv — the worker composition already standardizes on getKv(); sharing the handle yields durable + cross-replica dedup for free.

Port type WorkerIdempotencyPort + message idempotencyKey field live in @netscript/plugin-workers-core (storage-agnostic, type-only); concrete KvWorkerIdempotencyStore lives in plugins/workers/worker (which already imports @netscript/kv), keeping the publishable core free of a @netscript/kv dep — mirrors triggers' KvTriggerIdempotencyStore placement.

JobMessage.idempotencyKey is OPTIONAL; resolver precedence is caller key -> queue messageId -> sha256(payload) via crypto.subtle, so every inbound message has a key (doctrine 214) without breaking existing producers.
Worker.idempotency is a REQUIRED constructor dependency — no in-memory default is shipped; the KV store throws if the backend can guarantee neither atomic() nor a safe sequential durable path.
Stored key namespacing: ${concept}:${targetId}:${resolvedKey}; two KV spaces ['workers','idempotency','active'|'applied', key] with expireIn TTLs (active 15m, applied 24h, env-overridable).
Duplicate delivery yields a structured already-applied SKIP (span event + dedup metric), explicitly not a failure, per doctrine 216-218.
packages/queue is read-only context for this slice; queue/DLQ adapter changes are out of scope (owned by rbp-dlq-contract). No aspire/scaffold-versions/version-pin/@netscript/cli changes.
contracts: packages/plugin-workers-core/src/runtime/runtime-types.ts: JobMessage and TaskMessage gain `readonly idempotencyKey?: string`

packages/plugin-workers-core/src/ports/worker-idempotency-port.ts: new WorkerIdempotencyPort interface (claim/markApplied/release) + WorkerIdempotencyInput, WorkerIdempotencyClaim, WorkerIdempotencySource types
packages/plugin-workers-core/src/ports/mod.ts and src/public/mod.ts: re-export the new port + claim types

plugins/workers/worker/worker-options.ts: WorkerDispatchContext gains `readonly idempotency: WorkerIdempotencyPort`; WorkerOptions gains required `idempotency`; dispatchers accept queue MessageContext (messageId/deliveryCount)
testPlan: Unit (core resolver): caller>message-id>payload-hash precedence; deterministic sha256 of JSON-stable payload; namespacing ${concept}:${targetId}:${key}.

Unit (KvWorkerIdempotencyStore over @netscript/kv memory adapter): first claim succeeds; concurrent/duplicate claim returns claimed:false; release re-enables a fresh claim; markApplied sets applied marker so a later claim returns alreadyApplied:true; TTL expiry (expireIn) frees a stale active claim.

Integration (job-dispatcher): same message delivered twice (simulating kv-polling nack/visibility redelivery) creates exactly one execution and runs the effect once; second delivery returns already-applied skip with no executionState.create.

Failure-path (job-dispatcher): effect throws -> release(key) called -> a subsequent redelivery successfully re-claims and re-runs (no permanent wedge); already-applied is recorded as skip, not failure.
Integration (task path + queue-trigger path): processWorkerTask and listenToTriggerQueue/startTaskQueueListener are equally gated (no double execute on redelivery).

Producer (trigger-runtime-processor): enqueued JobMessage body carries idempotencyKey = action.options.idempotencyKey ?? event.idempotencyKey ?? event.id (assert on message, not just enqueue options).

Composition (service-runtime/bin runtime): the constructed Worker receives a KvWorkerIdempotencyStore bound to the same getKv() handle as executionState (durable + shared), asserted via injected KV spy.
Surface: deno publish --dry-run for @netscript/plugin-workers-core passes with the new type-only exports (no slow-type regression).
planGateSelfCheck: PASS expected on all eight boxes. Research present & re-baselined: every cited gap re-verified against current main with shifted line numbers recorded (job-dispatcher 17-127/130-196, worker.ts 244-275, runtime-types 134-161, producer 111-119/142, kv-polling 497-559); no cited gap is already resolved (both remain real). Decisions locked: 7 concrete locked decisions with rationale, zero deferrable rework. Open-decision sweep: queue-layer change marked safe-to-defer (separate slice owns it) and would not force rework; all rework-bearing choices (substrate, port placement, required dependency) are resolved now. Commit slices: 7 ordered, each names proof + gate + files. Risk register: present with mitigations. Gate set: chosen from archetype-gate-matrix for ARCHETYPE-3 + SCOPE-service, e2e:cli correctly excluded (no scaffold output change). Deferred scope explicit. jsr-audit: applied to planned public surface — new exports are interface/type-only, no slow-type risk, store kept out of publishable core. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: a reviewer could argue the slice meta lists packages/queue as a unit while the plan declares it out-of-scope — mitigated by an explicit research note proving the queue already exposes deduplicationId+deliveryCount and the defect is consumer-side, so no queue change is needed.
openQuestions: Slice meta lists packages/queue as a unit, but ground truth shows the queue already exposes deduplicationId (enqueue) and deliveryCount (consume); the plan treats packages/queue as read-only context. If PLAN-EVAL insists a queue-layer change is in-scope, it should instead be folded into rbp-dlq-contract — flagged, not blocking.

messageId stability across redelivery is guaranteed on the KvPolling adapter (same id re-nacked) and is the safe fallback; on adapters that mint a new id per delivery the caller idempotencyKey or payload-hash fallback governs. This is acceptable per doctrine (every message carries a key) but worth noting for adapter-specific guarantees.
risks: Adapters that assign a fresh messageId per delivery weaken the message-id fallback; mitigated because caller key (from the producer) is now stamped on the body and payload-hash is the final fallback, so dedup never depends solely on messageId.

KvStore.atomic is optional; a backend without atomic() must use the sequential has()+set() fallback which has a small TOCTOU window. Mitigated: active-claim TTL bounds the window and the store throws if no durable path exists, so we never silently ship non-durable dedup.

Making Worker.idempotency required is a breaking change to the Worker constructor; mitigated because all in-repo call sites (bin/runtime.ts, service-runtime composition, tests) are updated in the same slice and Worker is an internal plugin runtime class, not a stable published API consumed externally.

Trigger producer change touches plugins/triggers (a second plugin); mitigated by limiting it to a single additive field on the message body and a dedicated targeted test, with no change to trigger dedup or queue behavior.
Payload-hash fallback over large payloads adds a SHA-256 per delivery; negligible and matches the existing triggers implementation, only invoked when no explicit/message-id key exists.
